steps.title: Map the passage order
steps.content: The passage first introduces a feature shared by all baleen whales: filtering krill through baleen plates. It then compares humpback and fin whale size, which matters because body mass relates to how much krill a whale can consume. The structure moves from a broad feeding trait to a species-specific comparison tied to that trait.

steps.title: Reject reversed structures
steps.content: A reverses the order by putting the size distinction first. B adds population changes, and C calls baleen feeding unique to humpbacks, though the text says all baleen whales share it as a group. The answer is D.
